danger_signals.meaning: Combat imminent; player must prepare for danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger sprite size, distinct coloration, ornate design elements
danger_signals.meaning: Significant threat; elevated danger level requiring strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: Characters blocking pathways or facing player aggressively
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental obstruction; potential combat or dialogue barrier

safety_signals.visual_form: Populated NPC areas with domestic architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; merchants and healing services available

safety_signals.visual_form: Recognizable party members or friendly faction markers
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced threat; potential support or resource availability

safety_signals.visual_form: Tori gates, religious architecture, sacred symbols
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary location; healing and spiritual safety

faction_indicators.color_identity: Traditional Japanese color palette (greens, earth tones)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Historical samurai and ninja visual motifs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Japanese village structures, temples, traditional landscapes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanized enemy sprites distinguishing them from organic enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Futuristic or corrupted versions of traditional Japanese spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vibrant, intact Japanese landscape with active NPCs and commerce
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs engage in routine activities; no combat in safe zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental corruption; mechanical elements intruding into traditional spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy patrols; NPCs express alarm or flee; restricted access to areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mixed environmental aesthetics; conflict between organic and mechanical visual styles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Faction-aligned NPCs guard territory; enemy presence in formerly safe areas

environmental_storytelling.example: Traditional Japanese buildings showing signs of mechanical damage or corruption as Magginesu's influence spreads
NPCs relocating from invaded areas to safer zones; dialogue references to lost homes signal regional conquest
Recognizable locations appearing progressively altered by invasion forces, creating visual continuity of narrative progression
Increased visible presence of antagonist symbols and mechanized elements in conquered territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Aggressive sprite animation; directional facing toward player; movement patterns indicating pursuit
Centered screen positioning; distinctive attack animation sequences; extended health indicator
Stationary or wandering patterns; dialogue accessibility; no combat posture
Defensive stance; potential retreating animation; dialogue expressing worry about invasion

notes: Ganbare Goemon 2 uses traditional Japanese aesthetic contrast against mechanical/futuristic invasion imagery to communicate the core conflict. The Kiteretsu (nonsensical/bizarre) theme extends to visual presentation of Magginesu's forces as incongruous intrusions. World state progression is primarily communicated through environmental aesthetics and NPC behavior rather than explicit UI indicators typical of modern RPGs. Faction identity relies heavily on visual style consistency—organic vs. mechanical—rather than color-coding. This represents a paradigm where visual language communicates narrative progression and danger without text-heavy exposition.
